title: OT Simulator Environment Definition
description: Validates an environment.yaml file in design/environments/*/. An environment definition assembles device atoms onto network atoms to form a complete simulated OT facility, specifying IP addresses, Modbus ports, and network topology.

schema_version.description: Schema version for environment definition YAML files. Increment when breaking changes are made to the environment structure.

environment.description: Environment identity, classification, and narrative metadata.

id.description: Unique environment identifier matching the containing directory name (e.g., 'greenfield-water-mfg'). Used as the primary reference key for scenarios and documentation.

name.description: Human-readable environment name for display in the admin dashboard, scenario materials, and documentation.

description.description: Narrative description of the facility: industry sector, construction era, network architecture style, and OT security characteristics relevant to training scenarios.

archetype.description: Network architecture pattern. modern-segmented: Purdue model with VLANs, managed switches, and enforced tier boundaries. legacy-flat: single unmanaged network with no segmentation (common in facilities built before 2000). hybrid: mix of modern and legacy patterns typical of facilities modernized incrementally.

era_span.description: Year or year range representing the installation era of the facility's equipment (e.g., '1993-2024', '2015'). Communicates the coexistence of technology generations that characterizes real OT facilities.

networks.description: Network segments present in this environment. Every network referenced in placements, boundaries, bridges, or additional_networks must be listed here. Serial buses and WAN links are networks too.

items.description: Reference to a network atom by ID.

ref.description: Network atom ID matching the id field in a design/networks/*.yaml file (e.g., 'wt-level1', 'mfg-serial-bus'). Cross-reference validation (verifying the file exists) is performed by the CLI, not the schema.

placements.description: Device placements that instantiate device atoms onto network segments with specific addressing. Each placement defines one device instance in the environment.

boundaries.description: Network segmentation boundaries between network tiers. Describes the state of security boundaries: whether they are enforced in hardware, planned but not yet installed, or absent. Boundaries are optional -- some environments have no segmentation.

Placement.description: A device instance in the environment, wiring a device atom to a network segment with addressing and role assignment.

id.description: Unique placement identifier within this environment (e.g., 'wt-plc-01', 'mfg-gateway-01'). Used to reference this device instance in process schematics and scenario materials.

device.description: Device atom ID matching the id field in a design/devices/*.yaml file. Specifies which device type this placement instantiates. Cross-reference validation is performed by the CLI.

network.description: Primary network segment ID from the networks array. Ethernet placements specify an IP address on this network. Serial placements specify a serial_address and gateway referencing an Ethernet device on this network's corresponding parent segment.

ip.description: IPv4 address for this device on its primary network. Required for Ethernet-connected devices. Omit for serial devices (they have no IP address -- they are accessed via their gateway's IP).

modbus_port.description: Modbus TCP port number this device listens on. Standard OT port is 502; this simulator uses 5020-5059 to avoid privileged port requirements. The schema accepts any valid port (1-65535).

serial_address.description: Modbus RTU unit ID for serial-connected devices (1-247). This address is sent in the Modbus RTU frame to identify the target device on a multidrop serial bus. Serial devices do not have IP addresses -- they are addressed via the gateway's IP and this unit ID.

gateway.description: Placement ID of the serial-to-Ethernet gateway that provides IP connectivity for this serial device. The gateway's IP and Modbus port are used as the TCP endpoint; this serial_address identifies the device on the downstream RS-485 bus.

role.description: Human-readable description of this device instance's role in the facility (e.g., 'Water Treatment PLC 1 - Intake', 'Serial-to-Ethernet Gateway'). Used in HMI displays and scenario materials.

register_map_variant.description: Register map variant key from the device atom's register_map_variants object. Selects which role-specific register layout this placement exposes. If omitted, the device exposes empty registers within its declared capacity.

installed.description: Year this device was installed in the facility. Documents the installation era for educational narrative about technology generations coexisting in OT environments.

additional_networks.description: Additional network interfaces for dual-homed or multi-homed devices. A device may have presence on multiple network segments (e.g., a PLC with both a station LAN interface and a WAN-facing interface).

bridges.description: Network bridge definitions for gateway devices that connect two network segments. A gateway bridges protocol traffic between its Ethernet port and a serial bus, making serial devices accessible via the gateway's IP.

AdditionalNetwork.description: An additional network interface for a multi-homed device placement.

network.description: Network segment ID for this additional interface. Must be listed in the environment's networks array.

ip.description: IPv4 address for this device on the additional network segment. Omit for serial segments.

BridgeSpec.description: Defines a protocol bridge between two network segments on a gateway device. The gateway translates protocol traffic between its Ethernet port and a downstream serial bus.

from_network.description: Source network segment ID (typically the Ethernet segment where clients send Modbus TCP requests to the gateway).

to_network.description: Destination network segment ID (typically the serial bus segment where the gateway forwards Modbus RTU requests to downstream devices).

Boundary.description: A network segmentation boundary between two network tiers in this environment. Documents the security posture of inter-network boundaries, which is essential for OT threat modeling.

between.description: The two network segment IDs that this boundary separates. Exactly two segment IDs are required.

items.description: Network segment ID. Both IDs must appear in the environment's networks array.

state.description: Enforcement state of this boundary. enforced: boundary is installed and actively restricting traffic. intended: boundary is planned and appears in the design but is not yet installed in hardware -- a critical real-world OT pattern where budget approval precedes installation. absent: no boundary exists between these segments (flat network).

infrastructure.description: Type of boundary enforcement device. managed-switch: VLAN boundary enforced by a managed switch with 802.1Q trunking. firewall: stateful packet inspection appliance. ids-sensor: passive intrusion detection sensor (monitors but does not block). vlan-only: VLAN configuration without a dedicated firewall. other: custom or unspecified boundary infrastructure.

installed.description: Year this boundary infrastructure was installed. Used for era-based narrative in training scenarios.

notes.description: Free-text description of this boundary's history, security implications, and educational context for training scenarios.
